Identification.LongTitle: An Act to amend the Canada Elections Act (eliminating foreign funding)
Identification.ShortTitle: Eliminating Foreign Funding in Elections Act
Identification.RunningHead: An Act to amend the Canada Elections Act (eliminating foreign funding)

Provision.Text: This enactment amends the Canada Elections Act to broaden the prohibition on inducements of electors by non-residents and to clarify the meaning of âinduceâ. It also makes it an offence for a third party to accept a foreign contribution for any purposes related to an election and expands the list of foreign contributors in section 358.
Her Majesty, by and with the advice and consent of the Senate and House of Commons of Canada, enacts as follows:

Section.Text: This Act may be cited as the Eliminating Foreign Funding in Elections Act.
Section 331 of the Canada Elections Act is amended by replacing the portion before paragraph (a) with the following:

No person who does not reside in Canada shall, in relation to an election, in any way induce electors to vote or refrain from voting or vote or refrain from voting for a particular candidate or a candidate for a particular political party, including by promoting or opposing a political party or candidate, unless the person is

ExplanatoryNote.Label: Clause 2:
ExplanatoryNote.ExplanatoryText: Text of relevant portions of section 331:

Section.Text: No person who does not reside in Canada shall, during an election period, in any way induce electors to vote or refrain from voting or vote or refrain from voting for a particular candidate unless the person is
The Act is amended by adding the following after section 331:

For greater certainty, for the purposes of section 331, induce includes making a contribution to a person for the purpose of causing, directly or indirectly, that person to induce electors to vote or refrain from voting or vote or refrain from voting for a particular candidate or a candidate for a particular political party.
No third party, as defined in section 348.01, shall, at any time, accept a contribution for any purposes related to an election if the contribution is from any foreign source, including

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: a person who is not a Canadian citizen or a permanent resident within the meaning of subsection 2(1) of the Immigration and Refugee protection Act;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: a corporation or an association that does not carry on business in Canada;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: a trade union that does not hold bargaining rights for employees in Canada;

Paragraph.Label: (e)
Paragraph.Text: a foreign government or an agent of one;

Paragraph.Label: (f)
Paragraph.Text: a trust that is not resident in Canada; and

Paragraph.Label: (g)
Paragraph.Text: a partnership or joint venture, any of whose members, including another partnership or joint venture, are not resident in Canada.

Section.Text: For the purposes of sections 331.01 and 331.02, a contribution includes a loan.

Subsection.Text: Section 358 of the Act is amended by replacing the portion before paragraph (a) with the following:

Section.Text: No third party shall use a contribution for election advertising purposes if the contribution is from any foreign source, including:

ExplanatoryNote.Label: Clause 4:
ExplanatoryNote.ExplanatoryText: Text of relevant portions of section 358:

Section.Text: No third party shall use a contribution for election advertising purposes if the contribution is from

Paragraph.Label: (e)
Paragraph.Text: a foreign government or an agent of one.

Subsection.Text: Section 358 of the Act is amended by striking out âorâ at the end of paragraph (d) and by adding the following after paragraph (e):

Paragraph.Label: (f)
Paragraph.Text: a trust that is not resident in Canada; and

Paragraph.Label: (g)
Paragraph.Text: a partnership or joint venture, any of whose members, including another partnership or joint venture, are not resident in Canada.

Section.Text: Subsection 495(1) of the Act is amended by striking out âorâ at the end of paragraph (b), by adding âorâ at the end of paragraph (c) and by adding the following after paragraph (c):

ExplanatoryNote.Label: Clause 5:
ExplanatoryNote.ExplanatoryText: Text of relevant portions of subsection 495(1):

Subsection.Text: Every person is guilty of an offence who

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: contravenes subsection 326(1) or (2) (failure to provide election survey information) or, being a sponsor of an election survey, contravenes subsection 326(3) (failure to provide report on election survey results); or

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: contravenes section 327 (failure to indicate survey not based on recognized statistical methods).

Section.Text: Section 500 of the Act is amended by adding the following after subsection (6):

Subsection.Text: Every third party that is guilty of an offence under paragraph 495(1)(d) is, in addition to the punishment provided under subsection (1), liable to a fine equal to the amount of contributions accepted contrary to section 331.02.

Section.Text: This Act comes into force on the first anniversary of the day on which it receives royal assent or on an earlier day to be fixed by order of the Governor in Council.
